tool_args.label: Overtime overruns due to vacancy/absentee coverage

payload.claim_text: Overtime expense overruns for May and YTD were primarily due to vacancies and absentee coverage at NYCT, LIRR, MTA HQ, and MNR, and due to higher MTA PD deployment and vacancy/absentee coverage at MTA HQ.
payload.subject: MTA overtime expenses

evidence_refs.source_quote: Overtime expense overruns for May and YTD were primarily due to vacancies and absentee coverage at NYCT, LIRR, MTA HQ, and MNR, and due to higher MTA PD deployment and vacancy/absentee coverage at MTA HQ.

payload.claim_text: From January 2011 through February 2020, the twelve-month employment average increased 24.1%, while the 12-month MTA-wide ridership average fell 0.7%. The COVID pandemic has altered these trajectories: compared with January 2017, April employment is 5.0% higher, while MTA-wide ridership is 39.9% lower.
payload.subject: MTA ridership vs NYC employment
payload.description: Pre-pandemic and post-pandemic divergence between employment and ridership trends

evidence_refs.source_quote: From January 2011 through February 2020, the twelve-month employment average increased 24.1%, while the 12-month MTA-wide ridership average fell 0.7%.
